keto-dressing.name: Keto Dressing
instructions: Combine mayonnaise, olive oil, MCT oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, and garlic in a jar. Season with salt and pepper. Add chopped parsley; close the jar tightly with a lid and shake until well mixed.

kisses-peppermint-brownie-drops.name: Kisses Peppermint Brownie Drops

ingredients.item: Hershey's® kisses brand milk chocolates or hershey's® kisses brand candy cane candies

instructions: Stir together brownie mix, cocoa, and crushed peppermint candies in a large bowl. Add contents of syrup pouch, water, egg, and oil, beating until well blended. Refrigerate until firm enough to roll, about 1 hour.
Heat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper or lightly grease. Remove wrappers from candies.
Shape dough into 1-inch balls. Roll balls in powdered sugar and place on prepared cookie sheet.

Bake in the preheated oven until set, 9 to 11 minutes. Cool for 2 minutes. Press candy piece into the center of each cookie. Slide parchment paper and cookies to a wire rack or remove cookies from cookie sheet to a wire rack. Cool completely.

kiwi-muffins-baked-or-air-fryer-pequerec.name: Kiwi Muffins (baked Or Air Fryer)
kiwi-muffins-baked-or-air-fryer-pequerec.original_name: Magdalenas de kiwi (al horno o en freidora de aire)

ingredients.item: 70 ml soft olive oil or sunflower (paza)
150 g wheat flour (1 cup and ½ approx.)
Sugar tights or cuts of kiwi to decorate (optional)

instructions: We fight the kiwi and we smash them with the beaker till we get a purée.
In a bowl, we beat the eggs with sugar till the mixture bleeds.
We add oil and vanilla and mix
We put in the kiwi purée and remove well
We toss the flour with the yeast and salt, and we add it little by little to achieve a homogenous mass.
Let's put the mixture into muffins or muffins and fill up about 3 pieces.
We bake at 180 ″ 3 ″ for 18-20 minutes, till they are golden and as we pinch with a stick come out clean.

kuri-squash-falafel.name: Kuri Squash Falafel

ingredients.item: Red kuri squash or sugar pumpkin (about 6 ounces)
Shredded cabbage salad with pomegranate and tomatoes

instructions: Pulse chickpeas in a food processor, scraping down sides as needed, until they resemble finely chopped nuts (the texture should be uneven with some slightly larger pieces of chickpeas mixed throughout), about 1 minute. Scrape into a large bowl.

Pulse squash, onion, and garlic in food processor, scraping down sides as needed, until coarsely chopped, about 1 minute. Mix into chickpeas, then mix in chickpea flour, ras-el-hanout, salt, baking powder, pepper, and cinnamon. Form into ping-pong–size balls.

Pour oil into a large heavy pot to a depth of 3". Fit pot with thermometer and heat oil over medium-high until thermometer registers 330°. Working in batches, cook falafel, turning occasionally, until deep brown and crisp, about 5 minutes. Transfer to paper towels to drain and let sit 5 minutes.
Serve falafel with salad, tahini sauce, and pita.
Do Ahead: Falafel mixture can be made 5 hours ahead. Keep tightly covered at room temperature. When ready to fry, stir to loosen mixture and form into balls.
